info.name: tagalong
info.description:
REST API for tagalong — self-hosted continuous deployment for a single-node k3s cluster.

## Getting started
1. Set the `baseUrl` variable (defaults to `http://localhost:8080`).
2. Run **Auth → Login** first. The session is a `tagalong_session` cookie which Postman stores and sends automatically — no token setup needed. Fresh installs seed `admin` / `admin`.
3. Run **Apps → Create App**. Its test script saves `appId` and `webhookToken` collection variables, which the other requests use.

Everything under `/api` except healthz/login/logout/me requires the session cookie (401 otherwise).

variable.description: Must match the github_webhook_secret setting. Leave empty if unset (signature validation is skipped).

request.description: Liveness probe. Public, no auth. Returns {"status":"ok"}.

exec: // Session cookie (tagalong_session) is stored automatically by Postman.

request.description: Issues the tagalong_session cookie (7-day TTL). Response includes must_change_password when the default admin/admin password is still in place. Run this before any other request.
Returns the current session ({username, must_change_password}) or 401 if not logged in.
New password must be at least 8 characters. 403 if current_password is wrong. Re-issues the session cookie so the login stays valid.
Clears the session cookie.

name and image_repo are required. image_repo is normalized on save (e.g. `timdoddcool/robo-dash` → `docker.io/timdoddcool/robo-dash`). tag_strategy: exact | regex | latest | semver (default exact). strategy_conf per strategy: pattern (exact/regex), track_tag (latest, default "latest"), constraint (semver, e.g. ">=0.6").

The test script saves the returned id and webhook_token into the appId / webhookToken collection variables.
Full replace — send the whole app, not a partial patch. Same validation as create.
204 on success, 404 if the id doesn't exist.

Manual deploy of a specific tag: patches the target image to image_repo:tag and waits for the rollout (synchronous, up to 6 minutes — raise Postman's timeout if needed). Returns the DeployEvent; 502 if the rollout failed.
Empty body (no tag) = rollout-restart the current image. Needs imagePullPolicy: Always to actually pick up a re-pushed tag.
Generates a new per-app webhook token (invalidates the old Docker Hub webhook URL). The test script updates the webhookToken variable.
Live rollout status of each target workload, straight from the cluster.
Lists tags from the app's registry. 503 if the registry client isn't configured, 502 if the registry call fails.
All apps as one YAML document: {apps: [...]}. Server-managed fields (id, timestamps, last_seen_*) are omitted.

body.raw:
apps:
  - name: demo-app
    image_repo: docker.io/timdoddcool/robo-dash
    tag_strategy: exact
    strategy_conf:
      pattern: "^[0-9a-f]{7,12}$"
    targets:
      - namespace: default
        kind: Deployment
        name: demo-app
        container: web
    poll_enabled: false
    enabled: true

request.description: Upserts a {apps: [...]} document. Apps match by name (case-insensitive): existing → update, new → create; nothing is deleted. The whole document validates before any write. Omitting webhook_token on an existing app preserves its current token. Returns {created, updated, apps}.

body.raw:
name: demo-app
image_repo: docker.io/timdoddcool/robo-dash
tag_strategy: latest
strategy_conf:
  track_tag: latest
targets:
  - namespace: default
    kind: Deployment
    name: demo-app
    container: web
poll_enabled: true
poll_interval_sec: 300
enabled: true

request.description: Replaces a single app (keyed by id, so renames are fine) from a bare YAML document. Omitting webhook_token keeps the existing one.
Live Deployments/StatefulSets from the cluster — what the UI's "Browse cluster" uses to prefill a new app.

query.description: Pagination cursor: return events with id < before_id

request.description: Deploy history, newest first.

Server-Sent Events stream of deploy events (event: deploy_event). Note: Postman holds the connection open until you cancel — it won't render events incrementally like a browser. Useful mainly to confirm the endpoint responds; use curl -N for real streaming.
cloudflare_api_token comes back masked as ******** when set; github_webhook_secret is returned in the clear (operators need to paste it into GitHub).
Echoing the masked value ******** leaves that stored secret unchanged; send a real value to replace it, or "" to clear it.
Passwords come back masked as ********.
204 on success. Echoing password ******** keeps the stored password.
The path segment is the registry host (URL-encode if it contains special characters). 204 on success.

item.name: Webhooks
item.description: Public receivers (no session needed) — mounted at /hooks, not /api. They authenticate their own way: Docker Hub via the per-app token in the URL, GitHub via HMAC signature.

request.description:
Simulates a Docker Hub push webhook. The URL token identifies and authenticates the app (404 for unknown tokens). repository.repo_name is normalized to docker.io/<repo_name> and must equal the app's image_repo, or you get 400 "payload repo does not match app".

Responses: 202 {status: deploying} when the tag passes the strategy, 200 {status: skipped, reason} when it doesn't.

Run Apps → Create App (or Rotate webhook token) first so {{webhookToken}} is set.

exec: // Sign the body if a github webhook secret is configured.

request.description:
Simulates a GHCR registry_package publish. Matching is by normalized repo (ghcr.io/...) against a configured app's image_repo — to test end-to-end, create an app with image_repo ghcr.io/timothydodd/robo-dash.

Signature: if the githubSecret collection variable is set, the pre-request script computes the X-Hub-Signature-256 HMAC automatically (it must match the github_webhook_secret setting). If the server-side secret is empty, validation is skipped.

Responses: 202 deploying, 200 skipped/ignored ("no app for <repo>" for untracked packages), 401 invalid signature.
